question: Establish a multilevel inheritance hierarchy: `Electronics` (base class) -> `Smartphone` (derived from `Electronics`) -> `PremiumSmartphone` (derived from `Smartphone`). Each class should add at least one unique attribute (e.g., `Electronics` has `powerConsumption`, `Smartphone` adds `operatingSystem`, `PremiumSmartphone` adds `cameraMP`) and a method to display its specific details.

test_cases.expected:
--- First Premium Smartphone Details ---
Electronics Details:
  Power Consumption: 10.5W
  Manufacturer: ElectroCorp
Smartphone Details:
  Operating System: Android 12
Premium Smartphone Details:
  Camera Megapixels: 64MP

--- Demonstrating polymorphic deletion with non-virtual destructor ---
Electronics Details:
  Power Consumption: 12W
  Manufacturer: TechGear

Program finished. A memory profiler would indicate leaks due to design flaws.

errors: Missing virtual destructor in base classes (Electronics, Smartphone) leads to memory leaks during polymorphic deletion.

Using raw `char*` for string attributes without implementing Rule of Three/Five (copy constructor, assignment operator) can lead to shallow copies and undefined behavior (e.g., double-free) if objects are copied.
